<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Automorphic forms (adelic) — from $\Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}$ to $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$ 2 Strong approximation 3 Automorphic forms 4 Dictionary 5 Satake 6 Whittaker 7 $L$-functions

Automorphic forms, adelically

A classical modular form $f \in M_k(\Gamma_0(N))$ is a function on $\mathbb{H}$ with an $\mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{Z})$-equivariance. Reading the same data on the adelic group $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q})$ unifies every prime $p$ and the archimedean place into a single object, makes the Hecke action local at each prime, and turns $L$-functions into a global Euler product over places. This is the entry door to the Langlands programme.

blocks.html:
1. $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q})$ as a restricted product

Recall that the adèle ring $\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q} = \mathbb{R} \times {\prod_p}' \mathbb{Q}_p$ is the restricted direct product over all places of $\mathbb{Q}$, with the restriction $x_p \in \mathbb{Z}_p$ for almost every prime. Apply the matrix-group functor $\mathrm{GL}_2$ pointwise: $$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}) \;=\; {\prod_v}' \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_v) \;=\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R}) \times {\prod_p}' \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p).$$ The restriction at each finite place is membership in the compact open subgroup $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p) \subset \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p)$: $g_p \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p)$ for all but finitely many $p$. Equivalently, $g \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q})$ if and only if $\det g \in \mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}^\times$ and $g_p$ has entries in $\mathbb{Z}_p$ with $|\det g_p|_p = 1$ for almost every $p$.

Two factor split. A more practical decomposition for downstream use is $$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}) \;=\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R}) \;\times\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_f), \qquad \mathbb{A}_f := {\prod_p}' \mathbb{Q}_p,$$ separating the archimedean factor from the finite-adelic factor. The classical theory lives entirely on the $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})$ side; the level / Hecke / arithmetic data lives on the $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_f)$ side.

The standard maximal compact

The compact open subgroup $$K \;=\; \mathrm{O}_2(\mathbb{R}) \;\times\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\hat{\mathbb{Z}}) \;\subset\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}), \qquad \hat{\mathbb{Z}} \;=\; \prod_p \mathbb{Z}_p,$$ is the standard maximal compact. Inside the finite part, the relevant level-$N$ subgroup is $$K_0(N) \;=\; \Bigl\{\, g \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\hat{\mathbb{Z}}) \,:\, c \equiv 0 \pmod{N \hat{\mathbb{Z}}}\, \Bigr\}, \qquad g = \begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix}.$$ $K_0(N)$ depends only on the primes $p \mid N$: away from those primes it agrees with $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p)$.

Restricted-product membership tester
choose entries at each place; the widget tells you whether $(g_v)_v \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$
(any non-zero real)

See also
  • The adèle ring
  • Places and local fields

Used in
Automorphic forms (adelic) · Strong approximation and $\Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}$
</section>

2. Strong approximation and $\Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}$

The classical modular curve $Y_0(N) = \Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}$ has a clean adelic description. The bridge is strong approximation for $\mathrm{SL}_2$:

Strong approximation (one form). For any open compact $K_f \subset \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_f)$ that contains $\mathrm{SL}_2(\hat{\mathbb{Z}})$, $$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}) \;=\; \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \cdot \bigl( \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})^+ \cdot K_f \bigr),$$ where $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})^+$ is the connected component (positive determinant). The intersection $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \cap \bigl( \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})^+ \cdot K_f \bigr)$ is the classical congruence subgroup $\Gamma_f \subset \mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{Z})$.

Take $K_f = K_0(N)$. Then $\Gamma_f = \Gamma_0(N)$, and the chain of identifications is: $$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}) / \bigl( Z(\mathbb{R}) \cdot \mathrm{O}_2(\mathbb{R}) \cdot K_0(N) \bigr) \;\cong\; \Gamma_0(N) \backslash \bigl( \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})^+ / Z(\mathbb{R})^+ \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathbb{R}) \bigr) \;\cong\; \Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}.$$ The classical curve is a single double-coset space inside the adelic group.

Strong-approximation decomposition stepper
step through the factorisation of an adelic element

See also
  • Strong approximation
  • SL2(Z) action on the upper half-plane

Used in
Automorphic forms (adelic) · Adelic automorphic forms
</section>

<script>
/* ===== Section 2: strong-approximation stepper ===== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#strong-svg'), out = $('#strong-out');
  const prev = $('#strong-prev'), next = $('#strong-next'), reset = $('#strong-reset');

  const steps = [
    {
      title: 'Step 0: a generic g ∈ GL_2(A_Q)',
      caption: 'g lives in the full adelic group — one matrix per place, with restricted-product condition.',
      boxes: [{label:'g_∞', col:'var(--blue)', x:80, y:140}, {label:'g_2', col:'var(--violet)', x:200, y:140}, {label:'g_3', col:'var(--violet)', x:300, y:140}, {label:'g_5', col:'var(--violet)', x:400, y:140}, {label:'…', col:'var(--mute)', x:500, y:140}]
    },
    {
      title: 'Step 1: factor g = γ · h with γ ∈ GL_2(Q)',
      caption: 'Strong approximation says: there exists γ ∈ GL_2(Q) (diagonally embedded) and h with h_∞ ∈ GL_2(R)+ and h_p ∈ K_0(N) at every prime.',
      boxes: [{label:'γ ∈ GL_2(Q)', col:'var(--yellow)', x:140, y:60, w:180}, {label:'h_∞ ∈ GL_2(R)+', col:'var(--cyan)', x:140, y:200, w:180}, {label:'h_f ∈ K_0(N)', col:'var(--green)', x:380, y:200, w:180}]
    },
    {
      title: 'Step 2: project to the symmetric space',
      caption: 'GL_2(R)+ / Z(R)+ SO_2(R) = H, so h_∞ projects to a point τ ∈ H.',
      boxes: [{label:'γ ∈ GL_2(Q)', col:'var(--yellow)', x:140, y:60, w:180}, {label:'τ = h_∞ · i ∈ H', col:'var(--cyan)', x:140, y:200, w:180}, {label:'h_f ∈ K_0(N)', col:'var(--green)', x:380, y:200, w:180}]
    },
    {
      title: 'Step 3: quotient on the right by K_0(N)',
      caption: 'Right K_0(N)-coset of h_f is recorded; the remaining freedom is left multiplication by Γ_0(N) = GL_2(Q) ∩ (GL_2(R)+ · K_0(N)).',
      boxes: [{label:'γ ∈ Γ_0(N)', col:'var(--yellow)', x:140, y:60, w:180}, {label:'τ ∈ H', col:'var(--cyan)', x:240, y:200, w:180}]
    },
    {
      title: 'Step 4: classical Y_0(N) = Γ_0(N)\\H',
      caption: 'The double coset GL_2(Q) \\ GL_2(A) / (Z(R) · O_2(R) · K_0(N)) collapses to Γ_0(N) \\ H.',
      boxes: [{label:'Y_0(N) = Γ_0(N) \\ H', col:'var(--pink)', x:200, y:130, w:260}]
    },
  ];
  let i = 0;

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const s = steps[i];
    // title bar
    const t = SVG('text',{x:330, y:24, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill:'var(--ink)','font-weight':600});
    t.textContent = s.title; svg.appendChild(t);
    // boxes
    s.boxes.forEach(b => {
      const w = b.w || 80;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:b.x, y:b.y - 18, width:w, height:36, rx:6, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:b.col, 'stroke-width':1.6}));
      const tx = SVG('text',{x:b.x + w/2, y:b.y + 5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:b.col});
      tx.textContent = b.label;
      svg.appendChild(tx);
    });
    // caption
    const cap = SVG('text',{x:330, y:262, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent = s.caption.slice(0, 110);
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    if(s.caption.length > 110){
      const cap2 = SVG('text',{x:330, y:276, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      cap2.textContent = s.caption.slice(110, 210);
      svg.appendChild(cap2);
    }
    out.textContent = 'Step ' + i + ' of ' + (steps.length - 1) + ' — use ← / → to walk through the factorisation.';
  }

  prev.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ i = Math.max(0, i-1); draw(); });
  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ i = Math.min(steps.length-1, i+1); draw(); });
  reset.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ i = 0; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

3. Adelic automorphic forms

An automorphic form on $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q})$ is a function $$\phi : \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q}) \longrightarrow \mathbb{C}$$ satisfying three regularity conditions. Each is the natural adelic generalisation of a classical condition on $f \in M_k(\Gamma_0(N))$:

conditionclassical analogue
Right $K$-finiteness: the right-translates $\{\phi(\cdot k) : k \in K\}$ span a finite-dimensional space.Weight-$k$ modular forms transform by $(c\tau+d)^k$ — a finite-dimensional rep of $\mathrm{O}_2(\mathbb{R})$.
$\mathfrak{z}$-finiteness: $\phi$ is annihilated by an ideal of finite codimension in $Z(\mathfrak{g}_\mathbb{C}) = $ centre of the universal enveloping algebra.Holomorphy of $f$ is a Cauchy–Riemann eigen-condition for the Laplace–Beltrami operator on $\mathbb{H}$.
Moderate growth: $|\phi(g)| \ll \|g\|^N$ for some $N$, $\|g\|$ a height function.$f$ is holomorphic at the cusps (no $q^{-n}$ tail).
Cusp form. $\phi$ is a cusp form iff its constant term along the upper-triangular unipotent vanishes: $$\int_{\mathbb{Q} \backslash \mathbb{A}} \phi\!\left( \begin{pmatrix} 1 & x \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix} g \right) dx \;=\; 0 \qquad \text{for every } g \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}).$$ This is the adelic incarnation of "$f$ vanishes at every cusp of $\Gamma_0(N) \backslash \mathbb{H}$".

The space $\mathcal{A}(\mathrm{GL}_2)$ of all such $\phi$ is a $(\mathfrak{g}_\infty, K_\infty) \times \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_f)$-module, and decomposes (as a unitary representation of $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$ on $L^2$) as $$L^2_{\mathrm{disc}}\bigl( \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}) \bigr) \;=\; \bigoplus_\pi m(\pi) \cdot \pi$$ into a direct sum of irreducible automorphic representations. Multiplicity one for $\mathrm{GL}_2$ asserts $m(\pi) \in \{0, 1\}$ — and for cuspidal $\pi$ the multiplicity is exactly $1$.

Three conditions on $\phi$
click a row to highlight; the readout shows the consequence of dropping it
Click a row above to see what fails when that condition is dropped.

</section>

<script>
/* ===== Section 3: three-conditions clickable diagram ===== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#cond-svg'), out = $('#cond-out');
  const W = 660, H = 200;
  const rows = [
    { id:'kfin', label:'right K-finite', y:50, col:'var(--cyan)',
      explain: 'Without K-finiteness, φ would carry an infinite-dimensional K-representation — too large to admit a sensible decomposition into automorphic representations.' },
    { id:'zfin', label:'𝔷-finite', y:100, col:'var(--violet)',
      explain: 'Without 𝔷-finiteness, no Casselman–Wallach moderate-growth theory; the eigen-condition that turns differential operators into finite-rank algebraic data is gone.' },
    { id:'mod',  label:'moderate growth', y:150, col:'var(--yellow)',
      explain: 'Without moderate growth, φ might blow up faster than any polynomial near the cusps. Cuspidal Eisenstein and discrete series are precisely the moderate-growth pieces.' },
  ];
  let active = null;

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const phi = SVG('text',{x:80, y:H/2 + 6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':22, fill:'var(--yellow)','font-weight':700});
    phi.textContent = 'φ'; svg.appendChild(phi);
    const sub = SVG('text',{x:80, y:H/2 + 26,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    sub.textContent = 'GL_2(Q)\\GL_2(A) → C'; svg.appendChild(sub);
    rows.forEach(r => {
      const isActive = active === r.id;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:130, y1:H/2, x2:240, y2:r.y, stroke:r.col, 'stroke-width':isActive?2.4:1.2, 'stroke-opacity':isActive?1:0.6}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:240, y:r.y - 16, width:300, height:32, rx:6, fill:isActive?'rgba(125,224,214,0.10)':'var(--panel2)', stroke:r.col, 'stroke-width':isActive?2:1.4, style:'cursor:pointer'}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:390, y:r.y + 5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill:r.col, 'font-weight':600});
      t.textContent = r.label; svg.appendChild(t);
      const hit = SVG('rect',{x:240, y:r.y - 16, width:300, height:32, fill:'transparent', style:'cursor:pointer'});
      hit.addEventListener('click', () => {
        active = active === r.id ? null : r.id;
        draw();
        out.textContent = active ? r.explain : 'Click a row above to see what fails when that condition is dropped.';
      });
      svg.appendChild(hit);
    });
  }
  draw();
})();
</script>

4. The classical-to-adelic dictionary

Section 2's strong-approximation decomposition says each $g \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$ has a (non-unique) factorisation $g = \gamma \cdot g_\infty \cdot k_f$ with $\gamma \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q})$, $g_\infty \in \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})^+$, and $k_f \in K_0(N)$. Given a classical modular form $f \in M_k(\Gamma_0(N))$, define $$\phi_f(g) \;:=\; (f \big|_k g_\infty)(i),$$ where $f|_k g$ is the weight-$k$ slash operator $(f|_k g)(\tau) = (\det g)^{k/2} (c\tau + d)^{-k} f(g \cdot \tau)$ (and $\det g_\infty > 0$).

The function $\phi_f$ is well-defined: the ambiguities in the strong-approximation factorisation are exactly the elements of $\Gamma_0(N) \subset \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q})$, which act on $f$ by the modular automorphy factor — and on $i \in \mathbb{H}$ by an isometry of the maximal compact $\mathrm{SO}_2(\mathbb{R})$ — so the values agree.

The dictionary, summarised.
classicaladelic
$f \in M_k(\Gamma_0(N))$$\phi_f$ — automorphic form on $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A})$, weight $k$, level $K_0(N)$
cusp formvanishing constant term along the upper-unipotent
$T_p$ acts on $f$local Hecke algebra $\mathcal{H}_p$ acts on $\phi_p$
$L(s, f) = \sum a_n n^{-s}$$L(s, \pi) = \prod_v L_v(s, \pi_v)$
level $N$conductor of $\pi$, $\prod_p p^{c_p}$

Dictionary translator
pick a classical object and see its adelic counterpart

<script>
/* ===== Section 4: dictionary translator ===== */
(function(){
  const sel = $('#dict-sel'), out = $('#dict-out');
  const map = {
    form: 'Adelic side: φ_f : GL_2(Q)\\GL_2(A) → C, defined by φ_f(γ · g_∞ · k_f) = (f |_k g_∞)(i).\nKey property: φ_f is right-K_0(N)-invariant, K_∞-finite (weight k), of moderate growth.',
    cusp: 'Adelic side: cuspidal φ — the constant term ∫_{Q\\A} φ(n_x g) dx vanishes for all g, where n_x = ((1,x),(0,1)).\nThe adelic constant-term integral is the Whittaker-trivial Fourier coefficient.',
    hecke: 'Adelic side: at p ∤ N, the local Hecke algebra H_p acts on the K_0(p)-fixed space.\na_p = (α_p + β_p)·p^((k-1)/2) where (α_p, β_p) are the Satake parameters of π_p.',
    level: 'Adelic side: the conductor of π = ⊗ π_v.\nN = ∏_p p^{c_p}, where c_p is the smallest integer ≥ 0 with π_p^{K_0(p^{c_p})} ≠ 0 (Casselman).',
    lfn: 'Adelic side: L(s, π) = ∏_v L_v(s, π_v).\nUnramified p: L_p = (1 - α_p p^{-s})^{-1}(1 - β_p p^{-s})^{-1}.\nArchimedean: a gamma factor depending on the weight.\nMatches the classical Mellin transform of f.',
  };
  function show(){
    out.textContent = map[sel.value] || '';
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', show);
  show();
})();
</script>

5. The spherical Hecke algebra and the Satake isomorphism

Fix a prime $p \nmid N$, an unramified prime. The spherical Hecke algebra at $p$ is $$\mathcal{H}_p \;=\; C_c\bigl( \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p) / \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p) \bigr),$$ the algebra (under convolution) of compactly supported, bi-$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p)$-invariant functions on $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p)$. By the Cartan decomposition, every element of $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p)$ lies in a unique double coset $$\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p) \cdot \begin{pmatrix} p^a & 0 \\ 0 & p^b \end{pmatrix} \cdot \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p), \qquad a \ge b \in \mathbb{Z},$$ so $\mathcal{H}_p$ has a basis $\{\mathbb{1}_{a,b}\}_{a \ge b}$.

Local-to-classical Hecke

The element $T_p^{\mathrm{loc}} := \mathbb{1}_{1,0} + \mathbb{1}_{0,1}$ acts on the unramified part of an automorphic form, and unwinds (via the dictionary) to the classical $T_p$. Crucially, $\mathcal{H}_p$ is commutative: $(\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Q}_p), \mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p))$ is a Gelfand pair.

Satake isomorphism. There is an algebra isomorphism $$\mathcal{S} : \mathcal{H}_p \;\xrightarrow{\;\;\sim\;\;}\; \mathbb{C}\bigl[ X^{\pm 1}, Y^{\pm 1} \bigr]^{S_2}$$ onto the symmetric Laurent polynomials in two variables (the $S_2$ being swap of $X \leftrightarrow Y$, the Weyl group of $\mathrm{GL}_2$). Concretely, an unramified irreducible representation $\pi_p$ is determined by its Satake parameters $(\alpha_p, \beta_p) \in (\mathbb{C}^\times)^2 / S_2$; characters of $\mathcal{H}_p$ on the $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}_p)$-fixed line of $\pi_p$ are $$\mathcal{S}(T_p^{\mathrm{loc}})(\alpha_p, \beta_p) \;=\; p^{1/2}(\alpha_p + \beta_p).$$

The Satake parameters are the local incarnation of the Hecke eigenvalue. The classical eigenvalue $a_p(f)$ on a weight-$k$ eigenform reads $$a_p \;=\; (\alpha_p + \beta_p) \cdot p^{(k-1)/2},$$ and $\alpha_p \beta_p = p^{k-1}$ (or $1$ in the unitary normalisation), recovering the Euler factor $1 - a_p p^{-s} + p^{k-1-2s}$.

Satake parameter explorer
slide $a_p$; see the (unique up to swap) parameters $(\alpha_p, \beta_p)$ on the unit circle
0.00

See also
  • Commuting Hecke algebra

Used in
Automorphic forms (adelic) · Automorphic $L$-functions
</section>

<script>
/* ===== Section 5: Satake parameter explorer ===== */
(function(){
  const pSel = $('#sat-p'), kSel = $('#sat-k'), ap = $('#sat-ap'), apVal = $('#sat-ap-val');
  const svg = $('#sat-svg'), out = $('#sat-out');
  const W = 660, H = 280;
  const cx = W/2, cy = H/2 + 10, R = 90;

  function ramAdjust(p){
    const v = parseFloat(ap.value);
    return Math.max(-2*Math.sqrt(p), Math.min(2*Math.sqrt(p), v * Math.sqrt(p)));
  }

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const p = parseInt(pSel.value, 10);
    const k = parseInt(kSel.value, 10);
    const apv = parseFloat(ap.value);
    apVal.textContent = apv.toFixed(2);

    // Solve x^2 - (a_p / p^{(k-1)/2}) x + 1 = 0 for unitary Satake parameters on |x|=1
    const apTilde = apv / Math.pow(p, (k-1)/2);
    let alpha, beta, real;
    const disc = apTilde*apTilde - 4;
    if(disc < 0){
      // complex conjugate pair on |x|=1 (Ramanujan-conforming)
      const re = apTilde/2, im = Math.sqrt(-disc)/2;
      alpha = {re, im}; beta = {re, im:-im}; real = false;
    } else {
      const r1 = (apTilde + Math.sqrt(disc))/2, r2 = (apTilde - Math.sqrt(disc))/2;
      alpha = {re:r1, im:0}; beta = {re:r2, im:0}; real = true;
    }

    // unit circle (Ramanujan locus)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx, cy, r:R, fill:'none', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx-R-30, y1:cy, x2:cx+R+30, y2:cy, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx, y1:cy-R-30, x2:cx, y2:cy+R+30, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));

    // axis labels
    const xa = SVG('text',{x:cx+R+38, y:cy+4,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xa.textContent = 'Re'; svg.appendChild(xa);
    const ya = SVG('text',{x:cx+5, y:cy-R-32,'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    ya.textContent = 'Im'; svg.appendChild(ya);

    function plot(z, col, name){
      const x = cx + R*z.re, y = cy - R*z.im;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x, cy:y, r:5, fill:col, stroke:'#000','stroke-width':0.5}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:x+8, y:y-6,'font-size':12, fill:col,'font-weight':600});
      t.textContent = name; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    plot(alpha, 'var(--cyan)', 'α_p');
    plot(beta, 'var(--pink)', 'β_p');

    // title
    const t = SVG('text',{x:cx, y:24,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    t.textContent = 'Satake parameters live on the dashed unit circle when |a_p| ≤ 2 p^{(k-1)/2} (Ramanujan)';
    svg.appendChild(t);

    out.textContent =
      'p = ' + p + ',  k = ' + k + ',  a_p = ' + apv.toFixed(3) + '\n' +
      'Unitary Satake (α_p, β_p) on |z| = 1:\n' +
      '  α_p = ' + alpha.re.toFixed(4) + (alpha.im >= 0 ? ' + ' : ' − ') + Math.abs(alpha.im).toFixed(4) + ' i\n' +
      '  β_p = ' + beta.re.toFixed(4) + (beta.im >= 0 ? ' + ' : ' − ') + Math.abs(beta.im).toFixed(4) + ' i\n' +
      'α + β = ' + (alpha.re + beta.re).toFixed(4) + ' = a_p / p^{(k-1)/2}\n' +
      'α · β = 1  (unitary)\n' +
      (real ? 'Outside Ramanujan window: real roots, parameters off the unit circle.' :
              'Inside Ramanujan window: complex-conjugate pair on the unit circle.');
  }

  pSel.addEventListener('change', draw);
  kSel.addEventListener('change', draw);
  ap.addEventListener('input', draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

6. Whittaker model and the new-form

Fix a non-trivial additive character $\psi : \mathbb{Q} \backslash \mathbb{A} \to \mathbb{C}^\times$. A Whittaker functional on a representation $\pi$ is a linear $\Lambda : \pi \to \mathbb{C}$ satisfying $$\Lambda\!\left( \pi(n_x) v \right) \;=\; \psi(x) \, \Lambda(v), \qquad n_x = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & x \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix}.$$ For an irreducible admissible $\pi_v$ that is generic (= admits any such $\Lambda \ne 0$), the space of Whittaker functionals is one-dimensional — local multiplicity one.

Equivalently, the Whittaker model is the embedding $\pi \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Wh}_\psi$ that sends $v \mapsto W_v(g) := \Lambda(\pi(g) v)$. These functions $W_v : G \to \mathbb{C}$ are concrete, computable objects.

The new-vector

Casselman proved a remarkable structure theorem. Let $\pi_p$ be irreducible admissible, and let $c_p \ge 0$ be the smallest integer such that $\pi_p^{K_0(p^{c_p})} \ne 0$. Then:

  • $c_p$ is well-defined; called the local conductor exponent.
  • $\dim \pi_p^{K_0(p^{c_p})} = 1$ — there is a unique fixed vector up to scaling, the local new-vector.
  • For any $j \ge 0$, $\dim \pi_p^{K_0(p^{c_p + j})} = j + 1$.

The global conductor of an automorphic representation $\pi$ is $N(\pi) = \prod_p p^{c_p}$. For $\pi$ corresponding to a classical $f \in S_k(\Gamma_0(N))$, this $N(\pi)$ matches $N$ — the level. Atkin–Lehner new-form theory and Casselman's local theorem are two sides of the same statement.

Local conductor exponent ladder
click a level to see the dimension of $\pi_p^{K_0(p^j)}$
</section>

<script>
/* ===== Section 6: local conductor ladder ===== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#nf-svg'), out = $('#nf-out');
  const W = 660, H = 240;
  const cp = 2;  // toy local conductor exponent
  const levels = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
  let active = cp;

  function dim(j){
    return j < cp ? 0 : (j - cp + 1);
  }

  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const baseY = 180, dx = 90;
    levels.forEach((j, i) => {
      const x = 60 + i*dx;
      const d = dim(j);
      const isActive = j === active;
      // axis tick
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x, y1:baseY, x2:x, y2:baseY+8, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
      const lab = SVG('text',{x, y:baseY+24,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:isActive?'var(--yellow)':'var(--mute)','font-weight':isActive?700:400});
      lab.textContent = 'K_0(p^' + j + ')'; svg.appendChild(lab);
      // bar height = dim
      const barH = d * 24;
      const col = j < cp ? 'var(--mute)' : (j === cp ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--violet)');
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x-22, y:baseY-barH, width:44, height:Math.max(barH,2), fill:col, opacity:isActive?1:0.7, rx:3}));
      const dl = SVG('text',{x, y:baseY-barH-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:col,'font-weight':600});
      dl.textContent = 'dim = ' + d; svg.appendChild(dl);
      // hit
      const hit = SVG('rect',{x:x-30, y:baseY-90, width:60, height:120, fill:'transparent', style:'cursor:pointer'});
      hit.addEventListener('click', () => { active = j; draw(); update(); });
      svg.appendChild(hit);
    });
    // baseline
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30, y1:baseY, x2:W-20, y2:baseY, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.5}));
    // labels
    const t = SVG('text',{x:W/2, y:30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13, fill:'var(--ink)'});
    t.textContent = 'dim π_p^{K_0(p^j)} for an irreducible admissible π_p (conductor exponent c_p = ' + cp + ')';
    svg.appendChild(t);
  }
  function update(){
    const d = dim(active);
    out.textContent =
      'Selected level: K_0(p^' + active + ')\n' +
      'dim π_p^{K_0(p^' + active + ')} = ' + d +
      (active < cp ? '   ←  no fixed vectors (level too small)' :
       active === cp ? '   ←  the new-vector (unique up to scaling)' :
                       '   ←  ' + d + '-dimensional (oldforms span)') +
      '\n\nConductor exponent c_p = ' + cp + ' is the smallest j with non-zero fixed space.\n' +
      'Globally, N(π) = ∏_p p^{c_p} matches the classical level.';
  }
  draw(); update();
})();
</script>

7. Automorphic $L$-functions

To a cuspidal automorphic representation $\pi = \bigotimes_v \pi_v$ of $\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{A}_\mathbb{Q})$ one attaches an $L$-function $$L(s, \pi) \;=\; \prod_v L_v(s, \pi_v),$$ a product of local factors over all places. At an unramified $p$ with Satake parameters $(\alpha_p, \beta_p)$: $$L_p(s, \pi_p) \;=\; \det\!\bigl( I - \mathrm{diag}(\alpha_p, \beta_p) \cdot p^{-s} \bigr)^{-1} \;=\; (1 - \alpha_p p^{-s})^{-1} (1 - \beta_p p^{-s})^{-1}.$$ At ramified primes $p \mid N$, the factor is $(1 - a_p p^{-s})^{-1}$ with $a_p$ a single Hecke eigenvalue or, in worse cases, $1$. At the archimedean place, $L_\infty(s, \pi_\infty)$ is a product of gamma factors determined by the weight.

Match with the classical $L(s, f)$. For $\pi$ corresponding to a classical eigenform $f$, the unramified Euler factor unwinds to the classical Hecke factor: $$\bigl(1 - a_p p^{-s} + p^{k-1-2s}\bigr)^{-1} \;=\; (1 - \alpha_p p^{-s})^{-1}(1 - \beta_p p^{-s})^{-1},$$ where $\alpha_p + \beta_p = a_p$ and $\alpha_p \beta_p = p^{k-1}$ (in the classical normalisation). The full identity reads $L(s, \pi) = L(s, f)$ up to gamma-factor convention.

Why the adelic packaging is necessary

The story so far concerned $\mathrm{GL}_2$. Beyond it, classical holomorphic modular forms do not exist in the same sense — the symmetric space for $\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{R})$ at $n \ge 3$ is not a hermitian domain. The adelic formulation $$\phi : \mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{Q}) \backslash \mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{A}) \to \mathbb{C}$$ makes sense for every $n$, with the same three regularity conditions and the same Euler-product $L$-function. This is the gateway to the Langlands programme: a conjectural correspondence between automorphic representations of $\mathrm{GL}_n$ and $n$-dimensional Galois representations, with matching $L$-functions on both sides.

Local factor builder
build $L(s, \pi)$ place-by-place; toggle places to see partial Euler products
1.50

See also
  • Prototype L-functions
</section>

<script>
/* ===== Section 7: local-factor builder ===== */
(function(){
  const sEl = $('#lf-s'), sVal = $('#lf-s-val');
  const placesHost = $('#lf-places');
  const out = $('#lf-out');
  // toy Satake parameters for a weight-2 form (matches Δ-like profile rescaled to k=2 for visualization).
  // Use the actual Ramanujan Δ unitary parameters: α_p = e^{iθ_p}, β_p = e^{-iθ_p}, with τ(p)/2p^{11/2} = cos θ_p.
  // For pedagogy we use a small fixed list:
  const places = [
    { id:'inf', label:'∞ (archimedean)', factor: (s) => {
        // Γ_C(s) = 2(2π)^{-s} Γ(s)  — for weight-2 Δ-like.
        // approximate Γ via Stirling for s>0
        function gamma(x){
          if(x < 1) return gamma(x+1)/x;
          const c = [76.18009172947146,-86.50532032941677,24.01409824083091,-1.231739572450155,1.208650973866179e-3,-5.395239384953e-6];
          let y = x, t = x + 5.5; t -= (x + 0.5) * Math.log(t);
          let ser = 1.000000000190015;
          for(let j=0;j<6;j++){ y += 1; ser += c[j]/y; }
          return Math.exp(-t + Math.log(2.5066282746310005 * ser / x));
        }
        return 2 * Math.pow(2*Math.PI, -s) * gamma(s);
      }, default:true },
    { id:'p2', label:'$p = 2$', factor: (s) => 1/((1 - 0.5*Math.pow(2,-s))*(1 - 0.5*Math.pow(2,-s))), default:true },
    { id:'p3', label:'$p = 3$', factor: (s) => 1/((1 + 0.3*Math.pow(3,-s))*(1 + 0.3*Math.pow(3,-s))), default:true },
    { id:'p5', label:'$p = 5$', factor: (s) => 1/((1 - 0.7*Math.pow(5,-s))*(1 + 0.7*Math.pow(5,-s))), default:false },
    { id:'p7', label:'$p = 7$', factor: (s) => 1/((1 + 0.4*Math.pow(7,-s))*(1 + 0.4*Math.pow(7,-s))), default:false },
  ];

  const state = {};
  places.forEach(pl => {
    state[pl.id] = pl.default;
    const btn = document.createElement('button');
    btn.textContent = pl.label;
    btn.className = pl.default ? 'active' : '';
    btn.dataset.id = pl.id;
    placesHost.appendChild(btn);
  });

  placesHost.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if(e.target.tagName !== 'BUTTON') return;
    const id = e.target.dataset.id;
    state[id] = !state[id];
    e.target.classList.toggle('active', state[id]);
    update();
  });
  sEl.addEventListener('input', update);

  function update(){
    const s = parseFloat(sEl.value);
    sVal.textContent = s.toFixed(2);
    let prod = 1;
    let lines = ['s = ' + s.toFixed(3), '', 'Place         L_v(s, π_v)        running product'];
    places.forEach(pl => {
      if(!state[pl.id]) return;
      const v = pl.factor(s);
      prod *= v;
      lines.push('  ' + pl.label.replace(/\$/g,'').padEnd(14) + v.toFixed(5).padEnd(20) + prod.toFixed(5));
    });
    lines.push('');
    lines.push('Partial Euler product L(s, π) ≈ ' + prod.toFixed(5));
    lines.push('(toggle places above to grow the product)');
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  update();
})();
</script>
